text.title: serverless-architecture-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for serverless-architecture-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: The Lambdalith: stuffing the entire application into one Lambda function with a giant if/elif router for all routes and HTTP methods.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cannot scale routes independently. One failure affects everything. All routes share the same permissions and timeout. Deployment cycle touches every path on every change.

failure-mode.description: Nano-Functions: over-fragmenting into tiny single-operation functions (one per HTTP verb, one per database operation).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Debugging nightmare with deep call stacks. High latency from function-to-function synchronous calls. Business logic cohesion is lost across dozens of tiny units.

failure-mode.description: Synchronous Chains: Lambda A → sync → Lambda B → sync → Lambda C → sync → Lambda D, all blocking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Latency compounds linearly (100ms + 100ms + 100ms = 400ms+). Timeouts cascade. Cost doubles because Lambda A bills while waiting for B, C, D. Hard to trace and debug.

failure-mode.description: No DLQ: async Lambda invocations and SQS consumers without a Dead Letter Queue configured.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Failed events silently disappear. There is no way to replay, inspect, or alert on failures. Critical business events (orders, payments) are lost permanently on repeated failure.

failure-mode.description: No Idempotency: Lambda handler that charges a payment or sends a confirmation email without an idempotency key, despite being triggered from SQS or EventBridge.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lambda retries the same event on failure. Without idempotency the user is double-charged, double-notified, or data is duplicated.
